 {"id":"bd-1s9b","title":"Task: Implement proficiency config storage","description":"## Overview
Create configuration module for storing and retrieving user proficiency tier.

## Deliverables
1. Create internal/config/proficiency.go:
   - ProficiencyConfig struct with Tier, UsageStats, PromotionHistory
   - Load() - read from ~/.config/ntm/proficiency.json
   - Save() - persist to config file
   - GetTier() - return current tier (env override > config > default)

2. Configuration file format (proficiency.json):
   {
     "tier": 1,
     "usage_stats": {
       "commands_run": 150,
       "sessions_created": 25,
       "prompts_sent": 200,
       "first_use": "2025-01-01T00:00:00Z",
       "last_use": "2025-01-15T00:00:00Z"
     },
     "promotion_history": [
       {"from": 1, "to": 2, "at": "2025-01-10T00:00:00Z", "reason": "manual"}
     ]
   }

3. Environment override:
   - NTM_PROFICIENCY_TIER=1|2|3 overrides config
   - Useful for scripts that need specific tier behavior

4. Default behavior:
   - New users start at Tier 1
   - Create config file on first use

## Implementation Notes
- Use os.UserConfigDir() for cross-platform config path
- Handle missing/corrupted config gracefully (reset to defaults)
- Log tier changes for debugging

## Acceptance Criteria
- Config persists across sessions
- Env var override works
- Corrupted config recovers gracefully
- Unit tests for load/save cycle","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:24:21.511351381Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T06:05:42.202497506Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T06:05:42.202445949Z","close_reason":"Implemented proficiency config in internal/config/proficiency.go with Load/Save/GetTier, env override, usage stats, and unit tests","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1s9b","depends_on_id":"bd-7ny3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1s9b","depends_on_id":"bd-p3en","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1sa5u","title":"process_triage: Add [integrations.process_triage] config section","description":"Add configuration support for process_triage integration.

## Config Schema

```toml
[integrations.process_triage]
# Enable process health monitoring
enabled = true

# Path to pt binary
binary_path = ""

# Check interval in seconds
check_interval = 30

# Thresholds for classification alerts (seconds)
idle_threshold = 300      # 5 minutes
stuck_threshold = 600     # 10 minutes

# Actions on stuck detection
on_stuck = "alert"  # alert, restart, or ignore

# Enable rano correlation for better accuracy
use_rano_data = true
```

## Struct Definition

```go
type ProcessTriageConfig struct {
    Enabled        bool   `toml:"enabled"`
    BinaryPath     string `toml:"binary_path"`
    CheckInterval  int    `toml:"check_interval"`
    IdleThreshold  int    `toml:"idle_threshold"`
    StuckThreshold int    `toml:"stuck_threshold"`
    OnStuck        string `toml:"on_stuck"`
    UseRanoData    bool   `toml:"use_rano_data"`
}
```","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T07:21:46.390650147Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T22:43:09.239273765Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T22:43:09.239152136Z","close_reason":"Implemented ProcessTriageConfig with validation and unit tests","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1sa5u","depends_on_id":"bd-lzvh2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1sjqs","title":"Feature: Redaction integration across NTM IO (send/copy/save/mail/export)","description":"## Summary
Once the redaction engine exists, wire it into every place NTM can leak sensitive text.

## Targets
- `ntm send` + robot `--robot-send=...`
- `ntm copy` / `ntm save`
- `ntm mail send` (Agent Mail)
- checkpoint export/import (ensure uses shared engine)
- support bundles (separate epic)
- event log + prompt history writing paths

## UX
- Provide per-command overrides:
  - `--redact=off|warn|redact|block`
  - `--allow-secret` (explicit, scary override)
- Provide config defaults (per-project preferred).

## Acceptance Criteria
- All integrations share the same library.
- Robot mode returns stable error codes on block (e.g., `SENSITIVE_DATA_BLOCKED`).
- Human mode prints actionable remediation.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:21:25.115513244Z","created_by":"OrangeWolf","updated_at":"2026-02-01T03:21:25.122210070Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1sjqs","depends_on_id":"bd-27hn0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:48:38Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1sviv","title":"Unit Tests: All ntm assign strategies (balanced, speed, quality, dependency)","description":"## Test Coverage
Test all ntm assign strategy algorithms besides round-robin.

## Test Cases

### Balanced Strategy
- Distribute evenly when all agents have 0 assignments
- Prefer agents with fewer current assignments
- Handle tie-breaking (use pane order)
- Update scores after each assignment

### Speed Strategy
- Prefer agents with lower context usage
- Score = 1.0 - (context_pct / 100)
- Handle agents at 100% context (score = 0)
- Prefer fresh agents over loaded ones

### Quality Strategy
- Match bead type to agent capabilities
- Claude for refactoring/architecture
- Codex for feature/bug_fix
- Gemini for documentation/review
- Handle unknown bead types (default score)

### Dependency Strategy
- Prioritize high PageRank beads
- Prioritize high unblocks_count beads
- Score = pagerank * 0.5 + unblocks * 0.5
- Sort beads by score before assignment

### Strategy Selection
- Default to balanced
- Parse --strategy flag correctly
- Error on unknown strategy

## Detailed Logging Requirements (Go test)

### Test Structure
```go
func TestBalancedStrategy(t *testing.T) {
    t.Log("=== TestBalancedStrategy ===")
    
    // Setup
    t.Log("[SETUP] Creating test agents and beads")
    agents := []Agent{...}
    beads := []Bead{...}
    t.Logf("[SETUP] Agents: %d, Beads: %d", len(agents), len(beads))
    
    // Execute
    t.Log("[EXEC] Running balanced strategy")
    result := BalancedStrategy(beads, agents)
    t.Logf("[EXEC] Result: %d assignments", len(result))
    
    // Verify
    t.Log("[VERIFY] Checking distribution")
    for i, a := range result {
        t.Logf("[VERIFY] Assignment %d: bead=%s -> pane=%d, score=%.2f",
            i, a.BeadID, a.Pane, a.Score)
    }
    
    // Assert
    if len(result) != len(beads) {
        t.Errorf("[FAIL] Expected %d assignments, got %d", len(beads), len(result))
    } else {
        t.Log("[PASS] Assignment count correct")
    }
}
```

### Log Points per Test

#### Setup Logging
```go
t.Logf("[SETUP] Test: %s", t.Name())
t.Logf("[SETUP] Input agents: %+v", agents)
t.Logf("[SETUP] Input beads: %+v", beads)
t.Logf("[SETUP] Options: %+v", opts)
```

#### Execution Logging
```go
t.Log("[EXEC] Calling strategy function")
t.Logf("[EXEC] Strategy: %s", strategyName)
t.Logf("[EXEC] Duration: %v", elapsed)
```

#### Scoring Logging (verbose)
```go
t.Logf("[SCORE] Bead %s:", bead.ID)
for _, agent := range agents {
    t.Logf("[SCORE]   Agent %d (%s): score=%.2f", 
        agent.Pane, agent.Type, score)
}
t.Logf("[SCORE] Winner: pane %d with score %.2f", winner.Pane, winner.Score)
```

#### Assertion Logging
```go
// Before assertion
t.Logf("[ASSERT] Checking: %s", description)
t.Logf("[ASSERT] Expected: %v", expected)
t.Logf("[ASSERT] Actual: %v", actual)

// Result
if expected == actual {
    t.Logf("[PASS] %s", description)
} else {
    t.Errorf("[FAIL] %s: expected %v, got %v", description, expected, actual)
}
```

### Table-Driven Tests with Logging
```go
func TestStrategyScoring(t *testing.T) {
    tests := []struct {
        name     string
        strategy string
        agents   []Agent
        beads    []Bead
        want     []Assignment
    }{
        {"balanced_even", "balanced", ...},
        {"balanced_uneven", "balanced", ...},
        {"speed_fresh", "speed", ...},
    }
    
    for _, tt := range tests {
        t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
            t.Logf("[TEST] %s", tt.name)
            t.Logf("[INPUT] Strategy=%s, Agents=%d, Beads=%d",
                tt.strategy, len(tt.agents), len(tt.beads))
            
            got := RunStrategy(tt.strategy, tt.beads, tt.agents)
            
            t.Logf("[OUTPUT] Assignments: %d", len(got))
            for i, a := range got {
                t.Logf("[OUTPUT] [%d] bead=%s pane=%d score=%.2f",
                    i, a.BeadID, a.Pane, a.Score)
            }
            
            if !reflect.DeepEqual(got, tt.want) {
                t.Errorf("[FAIL] Mismatch\nwant: %+v\ngot:  %+v", tt.want, got)
            } else {
                t.Log("[PASS] Output matches expected")
            }
        })
    }
}
```

### Running with Verbose Output
```bash
go test -v -run TestBalancedStrategy ./internal/assign/...
```

## Test File
internal/assign/strategy_test.go

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All strategy algorithms have unit tests
- [ ] Tests log setup, execution, and verification steps
- [ ] Score calculations are logged in detail
- [ ] Failed assertions include context
- [ ] Table-driven tests cover edge cases","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T20:39:23.734216763Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T18:38:59.042650989Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T18:38:59.042567111Z","close_reason":"Created comprehensive strategy tests in internal/assign/strategy_test.go with detailed logging format. Tests cover balanced, speed, quality, dependency, and round-robin strategies. Fixed CLI assign_test.go type errors (paneInfo -> tmux.Pane). All tests pass.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1sviv","depends_on_id":"bd-k3zq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1sviv","depends_on_id":"bd-pjjk7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1syw","title":"Task: Register agents with Agent Mail on spawn","description":"## Purpose
Automatically register each spawned agent with Agent Mail for inter-agent communication.

## Scope
- On spawn, call register_agent for each non-user pane
- Use auto-generated adjective+noun names per Agent Mail conventions
- Store pane -> agent name mapping for session
- Create project-wide coordination thread

## Registration Flow
for _, pane := range panes {
    if pane.Type == AgentUser { continue }
    
    agent, err := am.RegisterAgent(ctx, RegisterAgentOptions{
        ProjectKey:      s.projectDir,
        Program:         'ntm',
        Model:           pane.Variant,
        TaskDescription: fmt.Sprintf('Agent in pane %s', pane.Title),
    })
    
    s.agentRegistry[pane.ID] = agent.Name
}

## Acceptance Criteria
- Agents registered with unique names
- Mapping stored for message routing
- Graceful handling when Agent Mail unavailable
- Registration survives session restart

## Technical Notes
Reuse existing agentmail client
Store mapping in session state","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

 {"id":"bd-1t881","title":"Test: Unit tests for LimitDetector pattern matching","description":"## Purpose
Comprehensive unit tests for LimitDetector pattern matching against actual agent output, covering all rate limit variations.

## Test Structure

```go
// internal/swarm/limit_detector_test.go
package swarm

import (
    "testing"
    "time"
)

func TestLimitDetector_PatternMatching(t *testing.T) {
    detector := NewLimitDetector(testLogger(t))
    
    // Test all known rate limit patterns
    patterns := []struct {
        name       string
        agentType  string
        output     string
        shouldMatch bool
        matchType   string
    }{
        // Claude Code patterns
        {
            name:       "claude_rate_limit_standard",
            agentType:  "cc",
            output:     "Error: rate limit exceeded. Please wait before trying again.",
            shouldMatch: true,
            matchType:   "rate_limit",
        },
        {
            name:       "claude_429_error",
            agentType:  "cc",
            output:     "API Error 429: Too Many Requests",
            shouldMatch: true,
            matchType:   "429",
        },
        {
            name:       "claude_token_limit",
            agentType:  "cc",
            output:     "You have exceeded your token limit for this billing period",
            shouldMatch: true,
            matchType:   "token_limit",
        },
        {
            name:       "claude_normal_output",
            agentType:  "cc",
            output:     "I'll help you implement that function. Let me start by...",
            shouldMatch: false,
        },
        
        // Codex patterns
        {
            name:       "codex_rate_limit",
            agentType:  "cod",
            output:     "openai.RateLimitError: Rate limit reached for default-gpt-4",
            shouldMatch: true,
            matchType:   "rate_limit",
        },
        {
            name:       "codex_quota_exceeded",
            agentType:  "cod",
            output:     "You exceeded your current quota, please check your plan",
            shouldMatch: true,
            matchType:   "quota",
        },
        
        // Gemini patterns  
        {
            name:       "gemini_resource_exhausted",
            agentType:  "gmi",
            output:     "google.api_core.exceptions.ResourceExhausted: 429 Resource exhausted",
            shouldMatch: true,
            matchType:   "resource_exhausted",
        },
        {
            name:       "gemini_quota_limit",
            agentType:  "gmi",
            output:     "Quota exceeded for GenerateContent",
            shouldMatch: true,
            matchType:   "quota",
        },
        
        // Edge cases
        {
            name:       "partial_match",
            agentType:  "cc",
            output:     "The word rate appears but limit does not follow immediately",
            shouldMatch: false,
        },
        {
            name:       "case_insensitive",
            agentType:  "cc",
            output:     "RATE LIMIT EXCEEDED",
            shouldMatch: true,
            matchType:   "rate_limit",
        },
        {
            name:       "multiline_output",
            agentType:  "cc",
            output:     "Processing...\nWorking...\nError: rate limit exceeded\nRetrying...",
            shouldMatch: true,
            matchType:   "rate_limit",
        },
        {
            name:       "empty_output",
            agentType:  "cc",
            output:     "",
            shouldMatch: false,
        },
    }
    
    for _, tt := range patterns {
        t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
            t.Logf("Agent: %s", tt.agentType)
            t.Logf("Output: %q", truncateForLog(tt.output, 100))
            t.Logf("Expected match: %v", tt.shouldMatch)
            
            match, pattern := detector.CheckOutput(tt.agentType, tt.output)
            
            t.Logf("Result: match=%v, pattern=%q", match, pattern)
            
            if tt.shouldMatch {
                assert.True(t, match, "Should detect rate limit")
                assert.NotEmpty(t, pattern, "Should identify pattern type")
                if tt.matchType != "" {
                    assert.Contains(t, pattern, tt.matchType, "Pattern type mismatch")
                }
            } else {
                assert.False(t, match, "Should NOT detect rate limit")
            }
        })
    }
}

func TestLimitDetector_CapturePane(t *testing.T) {
    // Create mock tmux client
    mockTmux := &MockTmuxClient{
        captureResponses: map[string]string{
            "cc_agents_1:1.0": "Normal agent output here",
            "cc_agents_1:1.1": "Error: rate limit exceeded",
            "cc_agents_1:1.2": "", // Empty pane
        },
    }
    
    detector := NewLimitDetector(testLogger(t))
    detector.tmux = mockTmux
    
    tests := []struct {
        paneID      string
        expectLimit bool
    }{
        {"cc_agents_1:1.0", false},
        {"cc_agents_1:1.1", true},
        {"cc_agents_1:1.2", false},
    }
    
    for _, tt := range tests {
        t.Run(tt.paneID, func(t *testing.T) {
            t.Logf("Capturing pane: %s", tt.paneID)
            
            output, err := detector.CaptureAndCheck(tt.paneID, "cc")
            require.NoError(t, err)
            
            t.Logf("Captured output length: %d", len(output))
            t.Logf("Limit detected: %v", tt.expectLimit)
            
            match, _ := detector.CheckOutput("cc", output)
            assert.Equal(t, tt.expectLimit, match)
        })
    }
}

func TestLimitDetector_BackgroundMonitoring(t *testing.T) {
    mockTmux := &MockTmuxClient{}
    detector := NewLimitDetector(testLogger(t))
    detector.tmux = mockTmux
    detector.pollInterval = 50 * time.Millisecond
    
    t.Log("Starting background monitoring")
    
    ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 200*time.Millisecond)
    defer cancel()
    
    eventCh := detector.Events()
    
    // Start monitoring
    go detector.StartMonitoring(ctx, []PaneSpec{
        {Index: 1, AgentType: "cc"},
    }, "test_session")
    
    // Inject rate limit after short delay
    go func() {
        time.Sleep(75 * time.Millisecond)
        mockTmux.SetCaptureResponse("test_session:1.1", "rate limit exceeded")
    }()
    
    // Wait for event
    select {
    case event := <-eventCh:
        t.Logf("Event received: %+v", event)
        assert.Equal(t, "cc", event.AgentType)
        assert.Contains(t, event.Pattern, "rate")
    case <-ctx.Done():
        t.Log("Context cancelled before event (expected if timing varies)")
    }
}

// Helpers
func truncateForLog(s string, max int) string {
    if len(s) <= max {
        return s
    }
    return s[:max] + "..."
}
```

## Logging Requirements
- Log each test case input (agent type, output snippet)
- Log expected vs actual match results
- Log pattern type when matched
- Log pane capture details
- Log background monitoring events

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Test all known Claude Code limit patterns
- [ ] Test all known Codex limit patterns
- [ ] Test all known Gemini limit patterns
- [ ] Test case insensitivity
- [ ] Test multiline output
- [ ] Test empty output
- [ ] Test partial/false matches
- [ ] Test pane capture integration
- [ ] Test background monitoring
- [ ] Mock tmux client for isolation","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-22T07:02:54.929888923Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T17:57:04.608090689Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T17:57:04.608007314Z","close_reason":"Added LimitDetector tests (all patterns, multiline, capture, monitoring); go test ./internal/swarm -run LimitDetector","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1t881","depends_on_id":"bd-3lo54","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1t881","depends_on_id":"bd-3un3t","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1uiu4","title":"Task: Unit tests for encryption library","description":"## Scope
Write comprehensive unit tests for the AES-GCM encryption helper.

## Cases
- Round-trip encrypt/decrypt with known key.
- Wrong key returns a typed/recognizable error.
- Corrupted header / corrupted ciphertext returns a typed/recognizable error.
- Nonce randomness: two encryptions of same plaintext differ.

## Logging
- Tests should log:
  - format version
  - ciphertext length
  - error types for negative cases

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests are hermetic and do not persist secrets.
- Uses only synthetic plaintext fixtures.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:48:39.966085602Z","created_by":"OrangeWolf","updated_at":"2026-02-04T20:00:22.588923069Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T20:00:22.588766198Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1uiu4","depends_on_id":"bd-29cp6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:48:38Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1uiu4","depends_on_id":"bd-z7ses","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:48:38Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":139,"issue_id":"bd-1uiu4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"DustyCastle: Added 10 new test cases covering nonce randomness, empty plaintext, unsupported version byte, data-too-short (3 sub-cases), invalid decrypt key, Error type methods (nil receiver, nil inner, with inner), IsKind with non-Error, EncryptFile/DecryptFile round-trip, and bad source paths. Coverage improved from 56.2% to 89.0%. All helpers and Error methods now at 100%.","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:33:57Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1unv","title":"EPIC: Agent Effectiveness Scoring and Learning","description":"**Agent Effectiveness Scoring and Learning EPIC**

## What It Does
Track agent effectiveness over time and use that data to improve assignments and workflows.

## Architecture (Existing + Planned)
- **Metrics definitions:** `internal/scoring/metrics.go` (core metrics + weights)
- **Score persistence + trends:** `internal/scoring/tracker.go` (JSONL, retention, trend windows)
- **Integration:** `ntm assign` + dashboard panel + robot/analytics export

## Data Sources (Deterministic)
- Task lifecycle signals (beads: assigned → in_progress → closed)
- Retry counts (re-sent prompts / reassignments)
- Time-to-complete (timestamps in task + session logs)
- Token usage (BudgetTracker / TokenEstimator)
- Quality gates (tests/CI pass, regressions)

## Learning / Scoring Rules
- **Cold start:** fallback to neutral baseline until minimum sample threshold met
- **Stability:** use rolling window + decay (recent scores weighted higher)
- **Fairness:** per-task-type normalization to avoid skew from harder tasks
- **No gaming:** cap extreme values, validate sources, log anomalies

## Success Criteria
1. Per-agent metrics tracked and persisted (JSONL)
2. Task completion rates calculated
3. Effectiveness scores computed deterministically
4. Scores influence recommendations with explainable rationale
5. Dashboard shows agent rankings + trends
6. Robot mode exports scoring data
7. Unit tests for scoring algorithm (>=80% coverage)
8. E2E tests verify scoring accuracy + recommendation influence

## Notes
- Retention defaults to 90 days; configurable
- Scores must be reproducible for the same input set","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

 {"id":"bd-1uunz","title":"Implement agent launch sequence after exit","description":"Implement agent launch sequence after successful exit.

## Steps
1. Send 'cc' Enter to pane
2. Wait for claude code initialization (6 seconds typical)
3. Verify claude code is ready (check for prompt or ready indicator)

## Timing
- cc command: immediate
- Initialization wait: 6 seconds
- Ready check: poll for up to 10 seconds

## Detection
Look for:
- Claude Code prompt character
- 'Claude Code' banner text
- Ready indicator in output

## Error Handling
- If cc command fails to start: return error with shell output
- If initialization times out: return error with pane content","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:25:12.846348928Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T02:32:48.358201896Z","closed_at":"2026-01-22T02:32:48.358098031Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1uunz","depends_on_id":"bd-21tqw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}

- Add a `[redaction]` section (or `[safety.redaction]`, whichever matches existing config style).
- Fields:

## Flags (canonical)
- `--redact=MODE` override per invocation.
- `--allow-secret` explicit override to bypass `block`.

- Config defaults apply consistently.

 {"id":"bd-214m","title":"Task: Query Agent Mail for recent threads","description":"## Purpose
Fetch recent Agent Mail messages for the session to include in recovery context.

## Scope
- Call FetchInbox with project_key and reasonable limit (10 messages)
- Include message bodies for context
- Filter to coordination threads related to this session
- Handle Agent Mail unavailable gracefully

## Query Parameters
FetchInboxOptions{
    ProjectKey:    s.projectDir,
    Limit:         10,
    IncludeBodies: true,
    SinceTS:       time.Now().Add(-24 * time.Hour), // Last 24 hours
}

## Acceptance Criteria
- Recent messages included in recovery context
- Message subjects and bodies formatted for injection
- Thread IDs preserved for continuity
- Graceful degradation when Agent Mail unavailable

## Technical Notes
Reuse existing agentmail client in internal/
Consider caching to reduce repeated queries","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T05:34:32.828893609Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T09:55:24.157947876Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T09:55:24.157905105Z","close_reason":"Implementation complete - loadRecoveryMessages() in spawn.go uses agentmail.FetchInbox with proper options","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-214m","depends_on_id":"bd-38kx","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}
-{"id":"bd-214u5","title":"Task: Integrate audit logging throughout NTM codebase","description":"## Overview
Add audit log calls at all significant action points in NTM.

## Deliverables
1. Integration points:

   CLI layer (internal/cli/):
   - Log every command execution
   - Include command args (sanitize secrets)
   - Log command result (success/failure)

   Session management (internal/session/):
   - Session creation with config
   - Session termination with reason
   - Agent spawn events

   Message sending (internal/agent/):
   - Prompts sent (preview, not full content)
   - Response received (summary)
   - Error conditions

   State changes:
   - Agent state transitions
   - Configuration changes
   - Resource allocation/release

2. Payload guidelines:
   - Never log full prompts (could be huge)
   - Include preview (first 100 chars)
   - Include length, token count estimates
   - Sanitize secrets/API keys

3. Correlation:
   - Generate correlation_id at command start
   - Thread through all related log entries
   - Enable tracing request flow

4. Performance considerations:
   - Async logging (don't block operations)
   - Batch writes for efficiency
   - Skip logging in test mode (configurable)

## Implementation Notes
- Create helper functions for common patterns
- Use structured logging consistently
- Consider log levels (debug, info, warn, error)

## Acceptance Criteria
- All significant actions logged
- Correlation IDs enable request tracing
- No sensitive data in logs
- Logging doesn't impact performance noticeably
- Unit tests verify logging at key points","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:33:42.660614881Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T18:34:05.216424152Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-214u5","depends_on_id":"bd-3fif","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-214u5","depends_on_id":"bd-3pbe","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-21tqw","title":"Feature: Robot Restart Pane Command (--robot-restart-pane)","description":"## Overview

Add `ntm --robot-restart-pane <session> <pane> [--bead=ID]` command that provides
atomic, reliable pane restart with all edge cases handled.

## Background

Restarting a stuck or rate-limited agent pane is currently a complex manual procedure:
1. Send Ctrl-C with precise 0.1s timing gap (too slow = won't exit)
2. Wait for shell to return
3. If soft exit fails, get child PID via pgrep and kill -9
4. Wait for shell prompt
5. Launch cc
6. Wait for initialization (6s)
7. Optionally send bead assignment prompt

This is error-prone and timing-sensitive. One wrong step and you have to start over.

## Proposed Interface

```bash
ntm --robot-restart-pane myproject 4 --bead=bd-3kmf
```

## Proposed Output

```json
{
  "session": "myproject",
  "pane": 4,
  "success": true,
  "exit_method": "soft",       // "soft" (Ctrl-C worked) or "kill" (needed kill -9)
  "old_pid": 12345,
  "new_pid": 54321,
  "agent_type": "claude",
  "bead_assigned": "bd-3kmf",  // null if --bead not provided
  "prompt_sent": true,
  "timing": {
    "exit_ms": 150,
    "restart_ms": 6200,
    "total_ms": 6350
  }
}
```

## Algorithm

```
1. Get current child PID (for verification)
2. Attempt soft exit:
   a. Send Ctrl-C
   b. Sleep 100ms
   c. Send Ctrl-C
   d. Wait up to 3s for process to exit
3. If soft exit failed:
   a. Get child PID via pgrep -P
   b. kill -9 <child_pid>
   c. Wait for shell prompt (up to 3s)
4. Verify shell returned (check pane output for prompt)
5. Send "cc" Enter
6. Wait for cc initialization (check for ready indicator)
7. If --bead provided:
   a. Construct bead assignment prompt
   b. Send prompt
8. Return structured result
```

## Flags

- `--bead=<ID>`: Assign this bead after restart (uses standard marching orders template)
- `--prompt=<TEXT>`: Send custom prompt instead of bead template
- `--no-verify`: Skip verification steps (faster but less safe)
- `--timeout=<MS>`: Override default timeouts

## Error Handling

- If pane doesn't exist: return error with available panes
- If exit fails after kill -9: return error with diagnostic info
- If cc fails to start: return error with pane output
- All errors include enough info to diagnose without manual inspection

## Why This Matters

This is the single most common recovery operation. Making it atomic and reliable
eliminates the #1 time sink in swarm management.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:15:39.223896546Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T06:22:48.760940110Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T06:22:48.760848938Z","close_reason":"Implemented --robot-restart-pane flag","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-21tqw","depends_on_id":"bd-258a1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"ntm-m961","title":"Implement routing strategies","description":"Multiple routing strategies for different use cases. Flexible work distribution.

STRATEGIES:

1. LEAST-LOADED (default):
   - Select agent with highest score
   - Best for balanced load distribution
   - Use when: general purpose, no task affinity needed

2. FIRST-AVAILABLE:
   - Select first agent in WAITING state
   - No scoring overhead - fastest
   - Use when: speed matters, agents are interchangeable

3. ROUND-ROBIN:
   - Rotate through agents in order
   - Ignores state/score completely
   - Use when: want predictable distribution

4. ROUND-ROBIN-AVAILABLE (New):
   - Rotate but skip busy/unhealthy agents
   - Combines fairness with availability
   - Use when: want fair distribution among available

5. RANDOM:
   - Random selection among available agents
   - Simple statistical distribution
   - Use when: don't want patterns

6. STICKY (New):
   - Prefer same agent for related tasks
   - Uses task affinity from prompt keywords
   - Falls back to least-loaded if affinity agent busy
   - Use when: context continuity matters

7. EXPLICIT:
   - User specifies exact pane
   - No routing logic
   - Use when: targeted send needed

STRATEGY INTERFACE:
```go
type RoutingStrategy interface {
    Name() string
    Select(agents []ScoredAgent, context RoutingContext) *ScoredAgent
}

type RoutingContext struct {
    Prompt      string    // for affinity matching
    LastAgent   string    // for sticky routing
    ExcludePanes []int    // explicit exclusions
}
```

EXCLUSION SUPPORT:
All strategies support --exclude-panes=1,3 to skip specific panes.

FALLBACK CHAIN:
If primary strategy finds no candidate:
1. Relax constraints (include THINKING agents)
2. Include rate-limited agents if backoff almost done
3. Return error if still no candidates

LOCATION: internal/robot/routing.go

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- All strategies implemented correctly
- Strategy interface consistent
- Round-robin maintains state across calls
- Sticky routing tracks affinity
- Exclusions respected by all strategies

 {"id":"ntm-matw","title":"T11: Fixed-width badge rendering for alignment","description":"# T11: Fixed-Width Badge Rendering for Alignment

## Parent Epic
[EPIC] Dashboard Layout Polish (ntm-1igj)

## Problem Statement
Model badges have variable widths causing visual misalignment:

\`\`\`
1 │ CC  │ ◐ work │ opus     │ ████░░ │  ← 4 chars
2 │ Cod │ ○ idle │ o1       │ ██░░░░ │  ← 2 chars
3 │ GMI │ ◐ work │ gemini-2 │ ███░░░ │  ← 8 chars
\`\`\`

The context bars don't align because model badges have different widths.

## Solution
Render badges at fixed width:

\`\`\`
1 │ CC  │ ◐ work │ opus     │ ████░░ │
2 │ Cod │ ○ idle │ o1       │ ██░░░░ │
3 │ GMI │ ◐ work │ gemini-2 │ ███░░░ │
                   ^^^^^^^^
                   Fixed 8 chars
\`\`\`

All model badges padded/truncated to 8 characters.

## Implementation Details

### Fixed Width Constants
\`\`\`go
const (
    ModelBadgeWidth    = 8
    PriorityBadgeWidth = 3  // P0, P1, P2
    StatusBadgeWidth   = 10 // "◐ working"
)
\`\`\`

### Badge Rendering
\`\`\`go
func RenderModelBadge(model string) string {
    // Truncate or pad to fixed width
    badge := model
    if len(badge) > ModelBadgeWidth {
        badge = badge[:ModelBadgeWidth-1] + "…"
    }
    
    return lipgloss.NewStyle().
        Width(ModelBadgeWidth).
        Render(badge)
}
\`\`\`

### Files to Modify
- \`internal/tui/dashboard/layout.go\` - Badge rendering
- \`internal/tui/components/badge.go\` - If separate badge file exists

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Model badges have consistent width
- [ ] Long models truncated with ellipsis
- [ ] Short models padded (left or right aligned)
- [ ] Column alignment is perfect
- [ ] Truncation threshold documented

## Testing Notes
1. Create session with various model types
2. Verify all model badges same width
3. Verify context bars align
4. Test edge cases: very long model names","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"LilacCastle","created_at":"2025-12-15T21:01:34.945149Z","updated_at":"2026-01-03T17:21:24.955541Z","closed_at":"2025-12-18T08:13:19.950565800Z","close_reason":"Added FixedWidth to BadgeOptions, width constants (ModelBadgeWidth=8), truncateBadgeText helper, and updated layout.go to use built-in fixed width instead of manual padding. Tests added.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":96,"issue_id":"ntm-matw","author":"jemanuel","text":"## Build-On-Existing Infrastructure Note

**EXISTING FIXED-WIDTH PATTERNS**:
- `badges.go:269` - `RankBadge` uses `Width(5)` for fixed alignment
- `badges.go:600` - `MiniBar` explicitly "fixed-width" for layout jitter

**WHAT THIS BEAD ADDS**:
- Extend fixed-width to MODEL badges specifically
- Constants for badge widths: `ModelBadgeWidth = 8`

**APPROACH**: Follow existing `Width(N).Align(Center)` pattern from RankBadge.","created_at":"2025-12-15T21:20:22Z"}]}
 {"id":"ntm-mjei","title":"Define profile schema and types","description":"## Overview
Define the Profile struct and TOML schema for agent profiles.

## Location
Create `internal/profile/profile.go`

## Schema Design
```go
type Profile struct {
    Name            string   `toml:"name"`
    AgentType       string   `toml:"agent_type"`       // claude, codex, gemini
    SystemPrompt    string   `toml:"system_prompt"`
    FocusPatterns   []string `toml:"focus_patterns"`   // glob patterns
    Tags            []string `toml:"tags"`             // for routing
    ModelPreference string   `toml:"model_preference"` // optional
    Description     string   `toml:"description"`      // human-readable
}

type ProfileSet struct {
    Name     string   `toml:"name"`
    Profiles []string `toml:"profiles"` // references to profile names
}

type ProfileConfig struct {
    Profiles    []Profile    `toml:"profiles"`
    ProfileSets []ProfileSet `toml:"profile_sets"`
}
```

## Validation
- Name must be unique, lowercase alphanumeric + hyphen
- AgentType must be valid (claude, codex, gemini)
- SystemPrompt should not exceed 4000 chars
- FocusPatterns must be valid glob syntax

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Profile struct with all fields
- [ ] ProfileSet for predefined combinations
- [ ] Validation functions
- [ ] Unit tests for schema validation","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","created_at":"2025-12-31T01:10:40.813898749Z","updated_at":"2025-12-31T05:18:32.422784994Z","closed_at":"2025-12-31T05:18:32.422784994Z","close_reason":"Implemented in persona package: FocusPatterns, PersonaSet, inheritance, and template variable resolution","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-mjei","depends_on_id":"ntm-j8t0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-31T01:11:11.823697493Z","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
